questions: How much protein do I need?
answers: Most adults need 0.8-1g of protein per kg of body weight daily. If you're active or building muscle, aim for 1.6-2.2g per kg. Good sources include eggs, chicken, fish, lentils, tofu, and dairy.
Top protein sources include chicken breast, eggs, fish, Greek yogurt, lentils, chickpeas, paneer, and whey protein. For vegetarians, combining dal with rice provides complete protein.

Protein timing matters less than total daily intake, but having some protein within a few hours of exercise can help muscle recovery. Spread your protein intake throughout the day across meals rather than consuming it all at once for better absorption.
